LAW AND MOTION 301, PETITIONER BERNAL HEIGHTS PARK ASSOCIATION'S MOTION FOR ORDER DETERMINING PREVAILING PARTY; MOTION TO TAX COSTS AND MOTION FOR AWARD OF COSTS. GIVEN THAT PETITIONER DID NOT OBTAIN A MONETARY REWARD OR THE RELIEF IT SOUGHT AND THAT RESPONDENT DID NOT ENTIRELY DEFEAT THE ACTION, NEITHER PARTY PREVAILED FOR THE PURPOSES OF SECTION 1032 AND IN ANY EVENT, THE COURT WOULD EXECISE ITS DISCRETION NOT TO AWARD COSTS TO EITHER SIDE. NOR HAS PETITIONER ESTABLISHED A RIGHT TO AWARD OF ATTORNEYS' FEES GIVEN ITS VERY LIMITED RELIEF, THE LACK OF A SUBSTANTIAL PUBLIC BENEFIT AND PETITIONER'S PRIVATE STAKE IN THE OUTCOME FOR WHICH IT ADVOCATED, BUT WHICH IT DID NOT ACHIEVE. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR# 8189

DECLARATION OF STEPHANIE M. WILLIAMS IN SUPPORT OF MOTION FOR ORDER DETERMINING PREVAILING PARTY; MOTION TO TAX COSTS AND MOTION FOR AWARD OF COSTS AND ATTORNEY'S FEES FILED BY PLAINTIFF BERNAL HEIGHTS PARK ASSOCIATION (AN UNINCORPORATED ASSOCIATION)

LAW AND MOTION 301, RESPONDENTS CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, SAN FRANCISCO BOARD OF SUPERVISORS AND SAN FRANCISCO RECREATION AND PARK COMMISSION'S MOTION TO STRIKE MEMORANDUM OF COSTS AND TO TAX COSTS. GIVEN THAT PETITIONER DID NOT OBTAIN A MONETARY REWARD OR THE RELIEF IT SOUGHT AND THAT RESPONDENT DID NOT ENTIRELY DEFEAT THE ACTION, NEITHER PARTY PREVAILED FOR THE PURPOSES OF SECTION 1032 AND IN ANY EVENT, THE COURT WOULD EXECISE ITS DISCRETION NOT TO AWARD COSTS TO EITHER SIDE. NOR HAS PETITIONER ESTABLISHED A RIGHT TO AWARD OF ATTORNEYS' FEES GIVEN ITS VERY LIMITED RELIEF, THE LACK OF A SUBSTANTIAL PUBLIC BENEFIT AND PETITIONER'S PRIVATE STAKE IN THE OUTCOME FOR WHICH IT ADVOCATED, BUT WHICH IT DID NOT ACHIEVE. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR# 8189

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Discovery Responses And To Impose Sanctions;Request For Judicial Notice
Off Calendar Aug-06-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Thomas S. Lakritz, Esq., appeared for Defendant & moving party: City & County of S.F.
No other appearance.

Ruling:
Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Respondents City and County of San Francisco, San Francisco Recreation and Park Commmission's Motion To Compel Discovery Responses is granted as discovery is allowed to determine the adequacy of Petitioner's allegations of a beneficial interest. In ruling upon a demurrer, the court found that Petitioners were required to plead that they had a beneficial interest. A party's standing and evidence of a beneficial interest would not be a part of the administrative record. The determination of standing and beneficial interest rests on the particular facts of each case. [See Braud v City Of Los Angeles (1990) 226 Cal.App.3d 83]
Responses shall be made within 15 days of notice of this order. No other objections to the discovery have been made by Petitioner.
Respondent's request for sanctions is denied.

Order signed in open court.
COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT

Off Calendar Nov-02-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: RESPONDENTS CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO BOARD OF SUPERVISORS, SAN FRANCISCO RECREATION AND PARK COMMISSION'S DEMURRER TO PETITIONER'S PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE.
APPEARANCE: Thomas S. Lakritz, Deputy City Atty 554-6547 for respondents & moving parties; no other appearances.
RULING: The Court did not allow any oral argument since respondents did not comply with the tentative ruling rule re giving proper notice to opposing party by telephone their intent to appear at the hearing. Therefore, the Court adopted its tentative ruling - OVERRULED AS TO CLAIMS RELATING TO STREET AND HIGHWAY CODE SECTIONS 8223 AND 8000. SUSTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO PUBLIC SERVICE EASEMENT CLAIM TO ALLEGE, IF PLAINTIFF CAN DO SO IN GOOD FAITH, THAT SOME RIGHT OTHER THAN A PUBLIC SERVICE EASEMENT HAS BEEN INFRINGED. Respondents may prepare form of order. (301/PJB/PM) Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR #8189
